_comment: Camera intrinsics for the depth feature (python -m src.depth).
EVERYTHING HERE IS OPTIONAL. MoGe-2 predicts the camera's field of view
itself, so depth estimation works with this file left exactly as it is.
Filling it in only makes the metric scale more accurate, because the model
is then told the true FOV instead of guessing it -- and a wrong FOV guess
translates directly into a wrong distance in metres.
Fill in EITHER fov_x_deg, OR fx together with width (fov_x is derived from
them). fy/cx/cy are not used for the depth map itself; they are stored here
for the planned 'distance and angle to an object' feature, which needs the
principal point to turn a pixel into a bearing.
Where the numbers come from: an OpenCV chessboard calibration
(cv2.calibrateCamera) gives fx, fy, cx, cy in pixels for a given capture
resolution -- record that resolution in width/height, since intrinsics are
only valid for the resolution they were measured at.
Point src.depth at a different file with --camera PATH or $CAMERA_CONFIG.
Values below are null = 'unknown, let the model estimate it'.
name: unnamed camera
